title: Timeline filter for Gantt chart
excerpt:

posts.author: JakiMonday (Jaki )
posts.cookedHtml:
I am trying to create a gantt chart to track campaigns. I have 2 issues.

  1. Many of my campaigns last a full year, so these are the campaigns that are coming up first. Is there a way to filter out certain campaigns (eg. if I create a status column for Year round campaign, can I remove the year-round items - but NOT the sub-items for the year round items?)

  2. If I only want to check out March campaigns (for example) can I restrict dates in the gantt to show March only?

Thanks

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Jaki,

You should be able to achieve this using our advanced filters in the Gantt view:

Screen Shot 2024-03-12 at 2.11.57 pm1220×606 44.1 KB

Is this along the lines of what you’re looking for?

posts.author: JakiMonday (Jaki )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bianca, it sort of does (I actually used this workaround) but I actually want the gantt to ONLY show March - with this solution I only have March campaigns, but all the other months are there which is not practical for the dashboard.
Thanks

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for getting back to me Jaki!

If you were to filter:

Timeline starts on this month
Timeline ends on this month

Does this help in pulling the one month in? I’ll do some testing on my end

posts.author: JakiMonday (Jaki )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bianca, It filters the items, but not the chart itself… unfortunately

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Ah, I see… I apologise for the setback here Jaki… I will relay this with our team internally and hope in the future we can make some relevant changes and improvements
